channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Anonymous food donations reach Indian student protesters
item.description: Anonymous donors are using food delivery apps to send food to student protesters in New Delhi

item.title: Trump push for Syrian intervention against Hezbollah ‘short-sighted’
item.description: Analysts say US president's pressure on Damascus to intervene in Lebanon could undermine domestic security.

item.title: Hamas’s new leader calls for permanent Gaza ceasefire
item.description: In his first speech since being elected Hamas political chief, Khalil al-Hayya called for a permanent ceasefire in Gaza.

item.title: Iran’s judiciary warns against ‘infiltrators’, announces new executions
item.description: Many cases related to security offences are prosecuted using a law announced after last year's 12-day war with Israel.

item.title: ‘Justice for Lorenzo’ protests disrupt Houston council meeting
item.description: Protesters shouted ‘Justice for Lorenzo’ at a Houston, Texas, city council meeting on Tuesday after an ICE shooting.

item.title: War on Iran: Hegseth wants $70B more to keep fighting
item.description: Iran activated air defence systems across Tehran on the 11th night of US strikes.

item.title: Proposed US-Saudi nuclear energy deal: What we know so far
item.description: Reports suggest ‌the Trump administration backs a pact with Saudi Arabia allowing it to enrich uranium.

item.title: Why are billionaires growing richer than ever?
item.description: Billionaires are on the rise as the rest struggle with inequality, poverty and cost of living crisis.

item.title: From towns to zones: Why Israel gives military names to civilian areas
item.description: Israeli authorities are increasingly applying security terminology for residential areas where people live.

item.title: Elon and the Other Gaza
item.description: How the world's richest man helped trigger an HIV crisis in one of the world's poorest countries.

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb Iranian bridges, power plants over ship attacks
item.description: Iranian official says Tehran would retaliate by targeting 'energy facilities in which the United States has interests'.

item.title: The Morocco-Algeria rivalry is remaking Europe’s North Africa policy
item.description: As Rabat and Algiers compete for influence, closer ties with one now carry consequences for relations with the other.

item.title: French model recruiter with close ties to Jeffrey Epstein found dead
item.description: Under investigation in France, Daniel Siad was accused of helping Epstein to traffic and abuse women.

item.title: Why Traore has opened a new front with Burkina Faso’s Muslim community
item.description: New legislation and arrests are driving a rift between Burkina Faso's military government and Muslim scholars.

item.title: Zelenskyy sacks Syrskii: How many generals have Ukraine, Russia fired?
item.description: Several Ukrainian and Russian generals have been sacked from their militaries over the course of the war in Ukraine.

item.title: Nearly 1,000 people have died from Ebola in DR Congo
item.description: According to WHO figures, 999 people have died in DRC and two in Uganda due to the Bundibugyo strain of the virus.

item.title: OpenAI says its AI model ‘went rogue’: What do we know?
item.description: The system hacked into another company without being prompted by a human.

item.title: UK’s Miliband, Streeting: What are their foreign policy, defence records?
item.description: Miliband, Trump critic who once opposed strikes on Syria, joins Streeting, defence novice with shifting Israel views.

item.title: Israeli strike leaves two Palestinians burnt beyond recognition
item.description: An Israeli strike hit a civilian vehicle in central Gaza, killing two Palestinians.

item.title: The Indian football tournament where goats replace trophies
item.description: GOATs are not players, but prizes at this football tournament in eastern India.

item.title: English FA charges Southampton coach Tonda Eckert over ‘spygate’ scandal
item.description: Allegations of spying on rivals led to Southampton's expulsion from the Championship playoff final.

item.title: ‘This land will remain ours’ says a resident in Lebanon’s ‘pilot zone’
item.description: ‘This land will remain ours’ says a resident in Lebanon’s ‘pilot zone’

item.title: ‘Just want to go back’: Lebanese await return as army enters ‘pilot zone’
item.description: Lebanese army deploys into southern Lebanon's Zawtar al-Gharbiya as implementation of US-brokered framework deal begins.

item.title: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine’s commander-in-chief after days of protests
item.description: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine’s commander-in-chief after days of protests

item.title: Settlers attack Palestinian towns in occupied West Bank
item.description: Settlers attack Palestinian towns in Occupied West Bank
